8zst8ean.label: The variability of organic carbon distribution within soil aggregates is quite large between cover crop treatments.

2wcbno33.label: When focusing on individual soil horizons, pairwise comparison indicates significantly higher mean weight diameter (MWD) compared to the fallow treatment for clover at 0-10 cm (18.8% higher, p=0.017), Mix12 at 20-30 cm (37.6% higher, p=0.049), and phacelia (17.0% higher, p=0.018) and Mix4 (12.8% higher, p=0.037) at 30-40 cm.

4rckdz4u.label: Mean weight diameter (MWD) of soil aggregates increases with soil depth and is significantly higher in the cover crop (CC) treatments than in the fallow treatment.

ct0dj54b.label: Mean weight diameter (MWD) of soil aggregates has the highest impact on aggregate organic carbon (OC) distribution, followed by soil parameters and cover crop (CC) type.

8awstpnz.label: Cover crops (CCs) increase the mean weight diameter (MWD) from 10% for oat and up to 19% for Mix12 compared to fallow.

1wyiidr1.label: Cover crop (CC) mixtures show a higher mean weight diameter (MWD) increase (16%) than single species (12%).
